title: Leverage extended thinking for architect-morty and security-morty
source: Anthropic Claude extended thinking feature (thinking blocks in API)
description: The architect-morty and security-morty roles handle complex reasoning tasks (architecture decisions, security analysis) that benefit from Claude's extended thinking capability. Extended thinking gives the model a dedicated reasoning scratchpad before responding, significantly improving output quality for multi-step planning and analysis tasks — exactly what these Opus-tier agents need.
proposed_changes: When delegating to architect-morty or security-morty (the Opus-tier agents in AGENT_MODELS), add `--thinking` or the equivalent extended thinking parameter to the claude subprocess call. In delegate.py's delegate_to_agent(), check if the model is 'opus' and the agent is in a reasoning-heavy role, then enable extended thinking. Update the agent JSON configs to include a 'use_extended_thinking: true' flag. Parse and optionally log the thinking blocks separately from the final output for debugging/audit purposes.
